title: Biography
bio1: Joan Fort (1997) is a Spanish guitarist and composer based in Amsterdam (NL). He holds a Master’s degree in Jazz Guitar from the prestigious Conservatorium van Amsterdam and Temple University in Philadelphia.
bio2: Throughout his career, Joan has had the privilege of touring, performing and collaborating with renowned musicians such as  Dick Oatts, Jeremy Pelt, Vincent Herring, Sarah Hanahan, Jesse Davis, Grant Stewart, Jorge Rossy, Bill McHenry, Benjamin Herman, Michael Weiss, Jasper Blom, Rita Payés and Virginia MacDonald.
bio3: His latest acclaimed record, Hangin' In (2026), was recorded in New York among a stellar line-up: Grant Stewart on tenor sax, Michael Weiss on piano, David Wong on bass, and Aaron Kimmel on drums.
bio4: One of his co-led projects, The Dam Jawn, has released two critically acclaimed albums: Master St (2023) and Forward (2024), both featuring the legendary New York saxophonist Dick Oatts. The first album earned a nomination for the Edison Jazz Award. In 2024, Joan performed with The Dam Jawn at the North Sea Jazz Festival, joined by the renowned trumpeter Jeremy Pelt as a special guest.
bio5: Joan has performed at prestigious festivals and venues worldwide, including the North Sea Jazz Festival (NL), Unterfahrt (DE), BIX (DE), Chris’ Jazz Café (USA), Bimhuis (NL), the Voll-Damm International Barcelona Jazz Festival (ES), and Windmills (IN).
